poll.title: 2027 Rule Change Ballot
poll.description: Ten proposed changes for next season. Each manager gets one ballot. Results update live after each valid submission.

questions.prompt: Keep the injured list cap at 10 slots?
questions.description: Current structure stays in place unless a majority wants a tighter roster environment.

questions.prompt: What should the minors cap be next season?
questions.description: This changes how aggressively teams can stash long-range prospects.

questions.prompt: Move the trade deadline later in the season?
questions.description: A later deadline gives contenders more runway but compresses the final market.

questions.prompt: Should FAAB increase from the current budget?
questions.description: Higher FAAB creates more tradeoffs during the season and can reward active churn.
Pitching volume rules shape how viable extreme ratios builds can be.
This would make it harder to keep fringe MLB contributors parked in minors slots.

questions.prompt: Change how the first-year player draft order is set?
questions.description: This covers whether to keep the current order logic or introduce a lottery element.

questions.prompt: Should payout weighting shift more toward the champion?
questions.description: Higher top-end payout increases title equity and reduces spread across the standings.

options.label: Make it slightly more top heavy
Make it much more top heavy

questions.prompt: Move to weekly lineup locks instead of daily moves?
questions.description: This is the largest format change on the ballot and would materially change roster management.

questions.prompt: Require a supermajority for future constitutional rule changes?
questions.description: A supermajority threshold makes structural rules harder to change once adopted.
